examples.title: Algorithm: Resource Allocation Graph & Cycle Detection
examples.description: Pseudocode for building a Resource Allocation Graph (RAG) and detecting cycles for deadlock identification
examples.code:
ALGORITHM ResourceAllocationGraph
INPUT: Processes P, Resource Types R with instances
OUTPUT: Deadlock detection result

BEGIN
 SET P = {P1, P2, ..., Pn} // Set of processes
 SET R = {R1, R2, ..., Rm} // Set of resource types
 SET requestEdges = {} // Process -> Resource (requesting)
 SET assignmentEdges = {} // Resource -> Process (assigned)

 // Function: Add Request Edge
 FUNCTION addRequestEdge(process Pi, resource Rj):
 IF Pi is not holding required resources THEN
 add edge (Pi --> Rj) to requestEdges
 RETURN "Request queued"
 ELSE
 RETURN "Request denied - process not ready"
 END IF
 END FUNCTION

 // Function: Add Assignment Edge
 FUNCTION addAssignmentEdge(resource Rj, process Pi):
 IF Rj has available instance THEN
 remove one instance from Rj
 add edge (Rj --> Pi) to assignmentEdges
 RETURN "Resource allocated"
 ELSE
 RETURN "No instance available"
 END IF
 END FUNCTION

 // Function: Detect Cycle (for single-instance resources)
 FUNCTION detectCycle():
 SET visited = {} // Track visited nodes
 SET recursionStack = {} // Track current path
 SET cycleFound = FALSE

 FOR EACH node in (P UNION R) DO
 IF node not in visited THEN
 IF DFS(node, visited, recursionStack) THEN
 cycleFound = TRUE
 BREAK
 END IF
 END IF
 END FOR

 IF cycleFound THEN
 RETURN "DEADLOCK DETECTED - Cycle exists in RAG"
 ELSE
 RETURN "NO DEADLOCK - No cycle in RAG"
 END IF
 END FUNCTION

 // Depth-First Search helper
 FUNCTION DFS(node, visited, recursionStack):
 visited.add(node)
 recursionStack.add(node)

 // Get all neighbors (edges from this node)
 SET neighbors = getNeighbors(node)

 FOR EACH neighbor in neighbors DO
 IF neighbor not in visited THEN
 IF DFS(neighbor, visited, recursionStack) THEN
 RETURN TRUE
 END IF
 ELSE IF neighbor in recursionStack THEN
 RETURN TRUE // Back edge found = cycle
 END IF
 END FOR

 recursionStack.remove(node)
 RETURN FALSE
 END FUNCTION
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the Resource Allocation Graph (RAG) structure used in OS deadlock detection. It shows: (1) How to represent request and assignment edges between processes and resources, (2) A cycle detection algorithm using DFS that identifies deadlocks in single-instance resource systems, and (3) The key insight that a cycle in RAG implies deadlock for single-instance resources.

examples.title: Python: Resource Allocation Graph Implementation
examples.description: Complete Python implementation of RAG with cycle detection and deadlock simulation
examples.code:
from collections import defaultdict
from typing import Dict, List, Set, Tuple

class ResourceAllocationGraph:
 """Represents the Resource Allocation Graph for deadlock detection"""
 
 def __init__(self):
 # Adjacency list representation
 # Graph nodes: processes (P1, P2...) and resources (R1, R2...)
 self.graph: Dict[str, List[str]] = defaultdict(list)
 self.all_nodes: Set[str] = set()
 
 # Track resource instances
 self.resource_instances: Dict[str, int] = {} # Resource -> num instances
 self.available_instances: Dict[str, int] = {} # Resource -> available
 
 # Track edges
 self.request_edges: Set[Tuple[str, str]] = set() # (process, resource)
 self.assignment_edges: Set[Tuple[str, str]] = set() # (resource, process)
 
 def add_resource_type(self, resource_name: str, instances: int):
 """Add a resource type with specified number of instances"""
 self.resource_instances[resource_name] = instances
 self.available_instances[resource_name] = instances
 self.all_nodes.add(resource_name)
 print(f"Added resource {resource_name} with {instances} instance(s)")
 
 def add_process(self, process_name: str):
 """Add a process node"""
 self.all_nodes.add(process_name)
 print(f"Added process {process_name}")
 
 def request_resource(self, process: str, resource: str) -> bool:
 """Process requests a resource""
 if process not in self.all_nodes or resource not in self.all_nodes:
 print(f"Error: Invalid process or resource")
 return False
 
 # Add request edge (process -> resource)
 self.request_edges.add((process, resource))
 self.graph[process].append(resource)
 
 # Check if resource is available
 if self.available_instances[resource] > 0:
 # Allocate immediately
 self.available_instances[resource] -= 1
 # Add assignment edge (resource -> process)
 self.assignment_edges.add((resource, process))
 self.graph[resource].append(process)
 # Remove request edge
 self.request_edges.discard((process, resource))
 self.graph[process].remove(resource)
 print(f"{process} acquired {resource}")
 return True
 else:
 print(f"{process} waiting for {resource} (not available)")
 return False
 
 def release_resource(self, process: str, resource: str):
 """Process releases a resource"""
 if (resource, process) in self.assignment_edges:
 self.assignment_edges.discard((resource, process))
 self.graph[resource].remove(process)
 self.available_instances[resource] += 1
 print(f"{process} released {resource}")
 else:
 print(f"Error: {process} does not hold {resource}")
 
 def detect_cycle_dfs(self, node: str, visited: Set[str], 
 recursion_stack: Set[str], path: List[str]) -> Tuple[bool, List[str]]:
 """DFS-based cycle detection"""
 visited.add(node)
 recursion_stack.add(node)
 path.append(node)
 
 for neighbor in self.graph[node]:
 if neighbor not in visited:
 found, cycle_path = self.detect_cycle_dfs(
 neighbor, visited, recursion_stack, path.copy())
 if found:
 return True, cycle_path
 elif neighbor in recursion_stack:
 # Cycle detected - extract cycle path
 cycle_start = path.index(neighbor)
 cycle_path = path[cycle_start:] + [neighbor]
 return True, cycle_path
 
 recursion_stack.remove(node)
 return False, []
 
 def detect_deadlock(self) -> Tuple[bool, List[str]]:
 """Detect if deadlock exists (for single-instance resources)"""
 visited = set()
 recursion_stack = set()
 
 for node in self.all_nodes:
 if node not in visited:
 found, cycle_path = self.detect_cycle_dfs(
 node, visited, recursion_stack, [])
 if found:
 return True, cycle_path
 return False, []
 
 def print_state(self):
 """Print current RAG state"""
 print("\n=== Resource Allocation Graph State ===")
 print(f"Processes: {[n for n in self.all_nodes if n.startswith('P')]}")
 print(f"Resources: {[n for n in self.all_nodes if n.startswith('R')]}")
 print(f"\nResource Instances: {dict(self.available_instances)}")
 print(f"Request Edges: {self.request_edges}")
 print(f"Assignment Edges: {self.assignment_edges}")


# Demonstration
if __name__ == "__main__":
 print("=== Resource Allocation Graph Demo ===\n")
 
 # Create RAG from textbook example (with deadlock)
 rag = ResourceAllocationGraph()
 
 # Add resources (single instance each)
 rag.add_resource_type("R1", 1)
 rag.add_resource_type("R2", 1)
 rag.add_resource_type("R3", 1)
 
 # Add processes
 rag.add_process("P1")
 rag.add_process("P2")
 rag.add_process("P3")
 
 print("\n--- Creating Deadlock Scenario ---")
 # P1 requests R1 (available, gets it)
 rag.request_resource("P1", "R1")
 # P2 requests R2 (available, gets it)
 rag.request_resource("P2", "R2")
 # P3 requests R3 (available, gets it)
 rag.request_resource("P3", "R3")
 
 rag.print_state()
 
 # Now create deadlock: each process requests another resource
 print("\n--- Creating Cycle (Deadlock) ---")
 rag.request_resource("P1", "R2") # P1 wants R2 (held by P2)
 rag.request_resource("P2", "R3") # P2 wants R3 (held by P3)
 rag.request_resource("P3", "R1") # P3 wants R1 (held by P1)
 
 rag.print_state()
 
 # Detect deadlock
 print("\n--- Deadlock Detection ---")
 has_deadlock, cycle = rag.detect_deadlock()
 if has_deadlock:
 print(f"DEADLOCK DETECTED!")
 print(f"Cycle: {' -> '.join(cycle)}")
 else:
 print("No deadlock detected")
examples.explanation: This Python implementation provides a complete RAG data structure with: (1) Resource and process management with instance tracking, (2) Request/release operations that automatically allocate available resources, (3) DFS-based cycle detection algorithm for single-instance resources, and (4) A demonstration creating the textbook deadlock example showing cycle P1→R1→P2→R2→P3→R3→P1. The demo creates a deadlock scenario identical to the textbook example.
examples.timeComplexity: O(V + E) for cycle detection where V=vertices, E=edges
examples.spaceComplexity: O(V + E) for graph storage and recursion stack

examples.title: C: RAG Cycle Detection with Adjacency Matrix
examples.description: C implementation of Resource Allocation Graph for deadlock detection using adjacency matrix
examples.explanation: This C implementation demonstrates: (1) Adjacency matrix representation of the RAG, (2) Separate tracking of request edges (process→resource) and assignment edges (resource→process), (3) DFS-based cycle detection with path reconstruction, (4) The textbook deadlock example with cycle P1→R1→P2→R2→P3→R3→P1. The implementation uses arrays instead of dynamic structures for educational clarity and compiles with any standard C compiler.
examples.timeComplexity: O(V²) using adjacency matrix, O(V+E) using adjacency list
